title: Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur
tagline: Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: The Chausathi Yogini Temple (64-Yogini Temple) of Hirapur, also said Mahamaya Temple, is 20 km outside Bhubaneswar, the capital of Odisha state of Eastern India.
overview.description: The Chausathi Yogini Temple (64-Yogini Temple) of Hirapur, also said Mahamaya Temple, is 20 km outside Bhubaneswar, the capital of Odisha state of Eastern India. It devotes to the worship of the yoginis, auspicious goddess-like figures.

topPlaces.name: Pratapsasan
topPlaces.description: Pratapsasan is a census town in Khordha district in the Indian state of Odisha.

topPlaces.name: Tosali
topPlaces.description: Tosali or Toshali was an ancient city in the present day Odisha state in eastern India. It was the capital of the eastern province of the Kalinga Kingdom.

topPlaces.name: Sisupalgarh
topPlaces.description: Sisupalgarh or Sisupalagada (Odia: [sisupaːl̪ɔɡɔɽɔ] ) is situated in Khurda District in Odisha, India, and houses ruined fortifications. First inhabited around 7th to 6th centuries BCE, it is one of the largest and best-preserved early historic fortifications in India, and was once the capital of ancient Kalinga.

topPlaces.name: Mahabhoi Sasana Tank
topPlaces.description: Mahabhoi Sasana Tank is situated on the right bank of the stream Gangua. It is mentioned as a tank in the forgotten monuments of Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India.

topPlaces.name: Madneswar Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: Madanesvara Siva Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Siva built around 12th century CE. It is situated on the left side of the Mahavir Lane branching from Garage Chowk in Santarapur to Sisupalgarh, Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India. The enshrined deity is a Siva lingam within a circular yonipitha (basement).

topPlaces.name: Brahmeswara Temple
topPlaces.description: Brahmeswara Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located in Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India, erected at the end of the 9th century CE, is richly carved inside and out. This Hindu temple can be dated with fair accuracy by the use of inscriptions that were originally on the temple.

topPlaces.name: Sarvatresvara Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: Sarvatresvar Siva Temple is situated on the right side of Mahavir lane branching from Lewis road to Sisupalgarh, Bhubaneswar, Orissa, India. It enshrines a Siva-lingam within a circular yonipitha inside the sanctum.

items.note: Pratapsasan is a census town in Khordha district in the Indian state of Odisha.
Tosali or Toshali was an ancient city in the present day Odisha state in eastern India. It was the capital of the eastern province of the Kalinga Kingdom.

Sisupalgarh or Sisupalagada (Odia: [sisupaːl̪ɔɡɔɽɔ] ) is situated in Khurda District in Odisha, India, and houses ruined fortifications. First inhabited around 7th to 6th centuries BCE, it is one of the largest and best-preserved early historic fortifications in India, and was once the capital of ancient Kalinga.
Mahabhoi Sasana Tank is situated on the right bank of the stream Gangua. It is mentioned as a tank in the forgotten monuments of Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India.

Madanesvara Siva Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Siva built around 12th century CE. It is situated on the left side of the Mahavir Lane branching from Garage Chowk in Santarapur to Sisupalgarh, Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India. The enshrined deity is a Siva lingam within a circular yonipitha (basement).

Brahmeswara Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located in Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India, erected at the end of the 9th century CE, is richly carved inside and out. This Hindu temple can be dated with fair accuracy by the use of inscriptions that were originally on the temple.

Sarvatresvar Siva Temple is situated on the right side of Mahavir lane branching from Lewis road to Sisupalgarh, Bhubaneswar, Orissa, India. It enshrines a Siva-lingam within a circular yonipitha inside the sanctum.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhubaneswar Railway Station (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur?
faq.a: Stays in Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chausath Yogini Temple, Hirapur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pratapsasan, Tosali, Sisupalgarh, Mahabhoi Sasana Tank, Madneswar Siva Temple.
